The food was absolutely outstanding â€” fresh, vibrant, and full of flavor. Everything tasted like it was made with care and quality ingredients, not the kind of meal you forget the next day. But what truly elevated our experience was our server, Dani. She was an absolute gem â€” warm, attentive, and genuinely enthusiastic about helping us navigate the menu. ReviewText:
I had their cauliflower rice bowl, got a side of plantains and had one of their juices. I canâ€™t remember what juice I got right now but it was clearly homemade and absolutely delicious. All I know it was a unique blend that I decided to get. Their plantains were fine but the Cauliflower Rice bowl needs more substance. I got a violent case of food poisoning from your guysâ€™ food.

They serve the kale and shredded carrots for this bowl raw. I donâ€™t necessarily think anything is wrong with shredded carrots but I believe itâ€™d be a better move if the kale was actually sautÃ©ed and given some substance. You arenâ€™t really given much nutrients with raw kale and also too, I wonder if the kale was properly washed (same for the carrots) and if the carrots are premade shredded carrots or shredded at the restaurant. Not sure what went wrong with the preparation but you definitely have to be careful and make sure hands are being washed properly and thereâ€™s no cross contamination.

I can say the same for the cauliflower rice and thatâ€™s a â€œriceâ€ that when not handled properly can spoil and cause issues. Maybe add some herbs to the cauliflower rice, sautÃ© or roast the cauliflower rice and give it some flavor to give some substance. The mushrooms couldâ€™ve been given more flavor too. I know for a fact that this is the dish that gave me food poisoning (day 2) of being in Costa Rica. I donâ€™t doubt that you guys have delicious food but this was a major miss. We went twice during our 5 day stay and would likely have gone a third time if we had stayed for longer.

Drinks: the strawberry mojito is delicious. Get it and thank me later.

Appetizers: we only tried the sushi tuna on crispy rice and it was wonderful. We ate tuna every day in Costa Rica and this may have been the best that we had although the Rip Jack tuna tower was a close second.

Entrees: we had the chifricoco, chicken teriyaki, and the lomito salteado hangar steak and all were delicious and full of flavor. My wife and I both agreed that the chicken teriyaki was about the best we have ever eaten.

Do not miss this place if you are staying anywhere in the Guanacaste region but do get there early. They close at 8pm.

ReviewText: I had the opportunity to enjoy breakfast at this restaurant along with my husband and it was an excellent experience from start to finish. Title: Disappointment
ReviewText: I rarely write reviews, but this experience left me genuinely upset and concerned. A few months ago, my partner and I visited this restaurant, hoping to enjoy a gluten-free meal in what looked like a beautiful setting. The atmosphere is nice, but that’s about the only positive thing I can say.From the moment we sat down, the service was terrible. The waitress (the receipt said her name was Fernanda) never introduced herself and seemed annoyed from the get-go. We asked about ingredients in some dishes, and it was immediately clear she had no idea what she was talking about not acceptable in a place that markets itself as gluten free.We both told her that we are celiac, and I also made it very clear that I have a lactose intolerance. Despite this, she brought us a banana pie that triggered both of our conditions. We spent the entire next day dealing with the consequences, and almost had to cancel an excursion we’d been planning for weeks. Completely unacceptable.This kind of negligence is dangerous. For people with dietary restrictions, eating out should not feel like playing Russian roulette. A place that claims to cater to gluten free diets must have properly trained staff  period. It’s truly disappointing because the food looked good and the place has potential. But until they take allergies and intolerances seriously and train their staff accordingly, I absolutely cannot recommend this restaurant  and I’ll be warning my friends to stay far away.

Title: Pots & Bowls: A Place That Feels Like Home
ReviewText: During our trip, we stumbled upon Pots & Bowls, and it turned out to be one of the best dining experiences we had. Not only is the food delicious, but the staff goes above and beyond to make you feel welcome. One night, after a long day of exploring the city, we arrived late—almost at closing time. Despite this, Kevin, Emily, and Fátima greeted us warmly and even made us a late-night coffee when everything was already cleaned up. It was such a thoughtful gesture that truly made us feel at home. We loved the experience so much that we decided to return the next day, and this time we were lucky to be taken care of by Kimberly and María Fernanda. They were just as kind and attentive, offering excellent recommendations and making sure we had another fantastic meal in a cozy atmosphere. If you're visiting the city, Pots & Bowls is a must-visit. Not only will you enjoy amazing food, but you'll also meet wonderful people like Kevin, Emily, Fátima, Kimberly, and María Fernanda, who make this place truly special.

Title: NOPE.
ReviewText: Don't bother...unless you like bad service and raw eggs. My husband ordered the potato hash bowl. The "fried" eggs were RAW (the white part was liquid) and it was covered with way too much of some disgusting sauce. He tried to eat it before he realized just how raw the eggs were. He was then no longer hungry. My burrito was okay, but it too had way too much sauce and I wouldn't say it came with rice & beans...mostly rice. The waitstaff are very friendly, but when it comes to doing the job of service they are either completely untrained or they simply do not care. The staff member that my husband finally managed to flag down offered to take the bowl back and cook it more. HUH?? He just wanted it out of his site. I asked for the bill twice, but we ultimately had to act as though we were leaving without paying in order to get someone's attention. We were extremely kind in our refusal of the raw food and yet they seemed not to care that our breakfast was ruined. As soon as we made them aware of the fact that we weren't happy they were done with us. And at the outrageous prices they charge...seems like something should justify the price. Nothing did. I have lived in Costa Rica for 10 years, so I have become quite accustomed to lowering my standards and paying higher prices, while foregoing complaining or reviewing. But, sometimes I just have to speak. The only reason I give them 2 stars is due to the wonderful saxophone player and the nice atmosphere.
